ticket.subject: Re: VA- Verified Address Feature for Aswini Homeo
ticket.raw_subject: Re: VA- Verified Address Feature for Aswini Homeo
ticket.description:
Hi Ram,

Thank you for sharing the details regarding the new Verified Address capability for Amazon Shipping in the India marketplace, along with the API documentation.

We are looping in our technical and support teams to review and take up the integration of this functionality. Based on our initial analysis, we are targeting an early June rollout for this feature, subject to successful implementation and testing.

As discussed over the phone, we kindly request your confirmation that once this feature is successfully rolled out, Aswni will process the full shipping volume through PluginHive with Amazon Shipping.

Our team will review the shared documentation in detail and will reach out if any further technical clarifications are required during the integration process.

Looking forward to your confirmation.

Thanks & Regards,

Vimal

Team PluginHive


> On 14 May 2026, at 12:53 PM, Shinde, Ram <ramshind@amazon.com> wrote:
> 
> Your attachment is now available for secure download. Amazon has replaced the attachment in this email with download link.
> Important: This link may contain content that is confidential. Please exercise appropriate caution when sharing.
> 1 Attachment Download all <https://us-west-2.secure-attach.amazon.com/all/c204d29e-3fe7-4451-9145-c523fe8594f5/97941639-d3d8-4312-bf06-40c3fa9e351e> Passcode required AZZSXy
> API Documentation.docx <https://us-west-2.secure-attach.amazon.com/97941639-d3d8-4312-bf06-40c3fa9e351e/7dbd988f-3a46-4f42-bcf5-32d431cbcfcf>
> Attachments Available Until: June 13, 2026, 07:23 (UTC+00:00)
> Help us improve: Share Your Feedback <https://amazonexteu.qualtrics.com/jfe/form/SV_ehuz6zGo8YnsRKK> Visit Here  <https://docs.secure-attach.amazon.com/guide>for FAQs and support resources.
> Dear Vimal,
>  
> We are introducing a new Verified Address capability as part of Amazon Shipping for the India marketplace, aimed at providing higher confidence on address deliverability and enabling better carrier allocation decisions for shippers. We would like to request your support in reviewing and incorporating this capability into your existing integration architecture for Amazon Shipping. As background, we are sharing the latest API Documentation – Verified Address, which outlines the detailed technical changes and expected behavior.
>  
> At a high level, the Verified Address capability includes the following:
> A new verification signal returned via the GetRates API as part of the benefits.includedBenefits array, specifically
> DESTINATION_ADDRESS_VERIFIED, indicating high deliverability confidence
> The feature is fully backward compatible; existing API requests remain unchanged
> Verification is best-effort, with graceful degradation when verification inputs are missing or the verification service is unavailable (the benefit will appear under excludedBenefits)
> No impact to core API SLAs, with circuit-breaker–based fallback behavior
> Applicable to the India (IN) marketplace only, with a planned availability starting 9th Feb 2026 for limited shippers under a controlled rollout
>  
> We request the integrator channel to:
> Review the attached API documentation in detail
> Assess and plan any required architecture changes to consume and propagate the new includedBenefits / excludedBenefits signals from GetRates API responses
> Align on handling of optional inputs, fallback behavior, and the dynamic (non-idempotent) nature of verification responses
> Incorporate and test the changes in sandbox ahead of the India marketplace rollout
>  
> Please let us know if you have any questions or need further clarification on the technical or integration aspects. We would be happy to set up a follow-up call if required.
>  
> Looking forward to your support and confirmation.
>  
> Regards,
> Ram Amrut Shinde
> Sales Manager
> E: ramshind@amazon.com <mailto:ramshind@amazon.com> | M: +91-9270010798
>  <https://shipping.amazon.in/>￼
image001.png

Our team will review the shared documentation in detail and will reach out if any further technical clarifications are required during the integration process.

Looking forward to your confirmation.

Thanks & Regards,

Vimal

Team PluginHive

comments.html_body:
Hi Ram,

Thank you for sharing the details regarding the new Verified Address capability for Amazon Shipping in the India marketplace, along with the API documentation.

We are looping in our technical and support teams to review and take up the integration of this functionality. Based on our initial analysis, we are targeting an early June rollout for this feature, subject to successful implementation and testing.

As discussed over the phone, we kindly request your confirmation that once this feature is successfully rolled out, Aswni will process the full shipping volume through PluginHive with Amazon Shipping.

Our team will review the shared documentation in detail and will reach out if any further technical clarifications are required during the integration process.

Looking forward to your confirmation.

Thanks & Regards,

Vimal

Team PluginHive


On 14 May 2026, at 12:53 PM, Shinde, Ram <ramshind@amazon.com> wrote:

Your attachment is now available for secure download. Amazon has replaced the attachment in this email with download link.
Important: This link may contain content that is confidential. Please exercise appropriate caution when sharing.
  • 1 Attachment
    •  
  • Download all
    •  
  • Passcode required AZZSXy
  • API Documentation.docx
Attachments Available Until: June 13, 2026, 07:23 (UTC+00:00)
Help us improve: Share Your Feedback Visit Here for FAQs and support resources.
Dear Vimal,
 
We are introducing a new Verified Address capability as part of Amazon Shipping for the India marketplace, aimed at providing higher confidence on address deliverability and enabling better carrier allocation decisions for shippers. We would like to request your support in reviewing and incorporating this capability into your existing integration architecture for Amazon Shipping. As background, we are sharing the latest API Documentation – Verified Address, which outlines the detailed technical changes and expected behavior.
 
At a high level, the Verified Address capability includes the following:
  • A new verification signal returned via the GetRates API as part of the benefits.includedBenefits array, specifically
    DESTINATION_ADDRESS_VERIFIED, indicating high deliverability confidence
  • The feature is fully backward compatible; existing API requests remain unchanged
  • Verification is best-effort, with graceful degradation when verification inputs are missing or the verification service is unavailable (the benefit will appear under excludedBenefits)
  • No impact to core API SLAs, with circuit-breaker–based fallback behavior
  • Applicable to the India (IN) marketplace only, with a planned availability starting 9th Feb 2026 for limited shippers under a controlled rollout
 
We request the integrator channel to:
  • Review the attached API documentation in detail
  • Assess and plan any required architecture changes to consume and propagate the new includedBenefits / excludedBenefits signals from GetRates API responses
  • Align on handling of optional inputs, fallback behavior, and the dynamic (non-idempotent) nature of verification responses
  • Incorporate and test the changes in sandbox ahead of the India marketplace rollout
 
Please let us know if you have any questions or need further clarification on the technical or integration aspects. We would be happy to set up a follow-up call if required.
 
Looking forward to your support and confirmation.
 
Regards,
Ram Amrut Shinde
Sales Manager
E: ramshind@amazon.com | M: +91-9270010798

comments.body:
Customer expects a functionality to identify orders that are best suited to be shipped via Amazon Shipping using the address validation API. While some addresses currently receive rates and labels from Amazon Shipping, return rates have been significantly high due to delivery quality issues in certain locations. The API is expected to confirm whether an address is fully serviceable/deliverable through Amazon, enabling us to provide an option to filter such orders and proceed with label creation only for highly deliverable shipments. This enhancement can help significantly reduce return rates and improve delivery success.

This feature is important to help win back the customer, Asawhi, who has indicated that they are willing to process their full shipment volume through Pluginhive once this functionality is successfully rolled out. The expected volume is approximately **50K shipments per month**.

Additionally, we are currently in the final stages of signing a partnership agreement with Amazon Shipping India to offer MCSL free of cost to their customers. Amazon has also committed to providing a consistent flow of customer referrals through their sales team.

Historically, delivery quality has been one of the key reasons for churn among Amazon Shipping customers. This feature could significantly improve customer retention and reduce churn by ensuring only highly deliverable shipments are processed through Amazon Shipping, ultimately improving delivery performance and customer satisfaction.
--Vimal
comments.html_body:
Customer expects a functionality to identify orders that are best suited to be shipped via Amazon Shipping using the address validation API. While some addresses currently receive rates and labels from Amazon Shipping, return rates have been significantly high due to delivery quality issues in certain locations. The API is expected to confirm whether an address is fully serviceable/deliverable through Amazon, enabling us to provide an option to filter such orders and proceed with label creation only for highly deliverable shipments. This enhancement can help significantly reduce return rates and improve delivery success.
 
This feature is important to help win back the customer, Asawhi, who has indicated that they are willing to process their full shipment volume through Pluginhive once this functionality is successfully rolled out. The expected volume is approximately 50K shipments per month.
 
Additionally, we are currently in the final stages of signing a partnership agreement with Amazon Shipping India to offer MCSL free of cost to their customers. Amazon has also committed to providing a consistent flow of customer referrals through their sales team.
 
Historically, delivery quality has been one of the key reasons for churn among Amazon Shipping customers. This feature could significantly improve customer retention and reduce churn by ensuring only highly deliverable shipments are processed through Amazon Shipping, ultimately improving delivery performance and customer satisfaction.
--Vimal
